title: Parag Parikh vs HDFC Flexi Cap Fund: Returns, risk, expenses, and portfolio holdings compared
summary: Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund currently leads the category with an AUM of ₹1.41 lakh crore, followed closely by HDFC Flexi Cap Fund at ₹1.01 lakh crore. While HDFC outperformed across the 1-year, 3-year, and 5-year periods, Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund delivered better returns over the 10-year horizon.

title: Mutual fund investing: 5 key ratios to check before choosing a fund

title: Book profits or stay invested at market highs? Historical data may settle the debate
summary: The analysis of 25 years of rolling market data suggests that remaining fully invested in equities gave better long-term returns rather than systematically transferring gains to debt after reaching pre-defined profit targets or market highs.

title: ITR filing AY 2026–27: What is AIS, how it differs from Form 16 and Form 26AS and how to download AIS?
summary: The Annual Information Statement (AIS) provides taxpayers with a comprehensive and consolidated view of their financial data. It includes the taxpayer's TDS and TCS details, specified financial transactions (SFT), tax payments, demand and refund records.&nbsp;

title: Gold and silver prices today, 15 June: Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold and 999 silver in Delhi, Mumbai, other cities
summary: Gold prices rose on 15 June &nbsp;due to optimism following a US-Iran peace deal. MCX gold for August futures increased by 1.71% to ₹1,53,490 per 10 grams, while silver rose 0.94% to ₹2,53,520 per kg amidst volatile equity markets.

title: Vedanta Oil and Gas shares list at Rs 39 on BSE as 4 demerged entities debut on Dalal Street
summary: Vedanta Oil & Gas shares debuted at Rs 39 on BSE and Rs 38 on NSE as part of Vedanta’s mega demerger that led to four new listings. The company’s valuation came broadly in line with estimates, marking a key milestone in one of India’s largest corporate restructuring exercises.

title: Nuvama Wealth among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
summary: Five NSE F&O stocks recorded over 6% growth in futures open interest on June 12, indicating rising trader participation and fresh positioning. Nuvama Wealth, Radico Khaitan and Hindalco led the increase.

title: Vedanta Iron & Steel shares list at Rs 22 on BSE as mega demerger concludes
summary: Vedanta Iron & Steel debuted on the BSE at Rs 22 and NSE at Rs 20 on Monday as part of Vedanta's demerger plan. The company, with operations in India and Africa, focuses on iron ore and steel production. Its market capitalization at debut was approximately Rs 7,821 crore.
